entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A valuable staple crop grown in semi-arid ecologies of drylands in Asia and Africa, providing food for humans and feed for livestock.

entities.name: GENETIC GAINS
entities.description: The increase in the genetic merit of a crop population over time through selection and breeding.

entities.name: RESILIENCE
entities.description: The capacity of a crop to withstand or recover from adverse environmental conditions.

entities.name: CLIMATIC STRESS
entities.description: Environmental pressures resulting from climate variability and change that affect crop growth.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID ECOLOGIES
entities.description: Environmental regions characterized by low and irregular precipitation.

entities.name: DRYLANDS
entities.description: Terrestrial regions with low annual rainfall and high evaporation rates.

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: A continent where pearl millet is cultivated in dryland regions.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A continent where pearl millet is cultivated in dryland regions.

entities.name: HUMANS
entities.description: The primary consumers of pearl millet as a staple food source.

entities.name: LIVESTOCK
entities.description: Animals that utilize the stover of pearl millet as a source of feed.

entities.name: STOVER
entities.description: The vegetative parts of the pearl millet plant remaining after grain harvest, used as animal fodder.
